Lisinopril.management: The dry cough is the most common reason to switch to an ARB (like losartan). Rise slowly from sitting/lying. Report any facial swelling immediately — it is a medical emergency.

serious: Vitamin B12 deficiency with long-term use
Metformin.management: GI side effects usually improve after 2-4 weeks. Take with food. Extended-release formulation causes fewer GI side effects. Get B12 levels checked annually.

Atorvastatin.management: Report any unexplained muscle pain, weakness, or dark urine immediately. Avoid grapefruit juice. Liver function tests recommended before starting.

Amlodipine.management: Ankle swelling is common and usually harmless — elevating legs helps. Flushing and headache usually improve after a few weeks.

Metoprolol.management: Never stop abruptly — can cause rebound hypertension or angina. Monitor pulse. Avoid in asthma.

serious: Increased suicidal thoughts (especially in young adults, first weeks)
Sertraline.management: Takes 2-4 weeks for full antidepressant effect. Take with food for nausea. Do not stop abruptly — taper over weeks.

Omeprazole.management: Take 30-60 minutes before first meal. Reassess need every 3-6 months. Long-term use should be medically justified.

Gabapentin.management: Start low and increase slowly. Avoid alcohol and other CNS depressants. Do not drive until you know how it affects you.

Albuterol.management: Shake inhaler before use. Rinse mouth after use. If needed more than 2 days/week, asthma is not well-controlled — see doctor.
